First, Matthew worked from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM yesterday. That's 10 hours in total. His ranking for riding distance, money earned, and number of orders were all 12th out of 100. So he's performing decently but not top tier.

I wonder if the time he started and finished work is optimal. Maybe starting earlier or later could affect his performance. Since he was in the middle rank, perhaps adjusting his hours might help improve his stats.

If he starts earlier, say at 7:00 AM, he can pick up more orders during the morning rush. This might increase both the number of orders and the distance ridden, potentially boosting his earnings. Ending later, around 19:00 (7 PM), could capture evening demand as well.

Alternatively, maybe cutting down on hours but focusing on peak times could be better. But given that he's in the middle, extending hours seems like it might help him catch up with higher-ranked riders who probably work longer or during busier periods.

So, changing his working hours to start earlier and finish later could give him more opportunities to deliver orders, which should improve his rankings. Therefore, starting at 7:00 AM and ending at 19:00 seems like a good adjustment.

After weighing these factors, it seems that adjusting my working hours to start later and end earlier might improve my efficiency and earnings without sacrificing too much in terms of distance or orders. This change could help me maintain a better work-life balance and potentially increase my earning rank by focusing on higher-paying orders during peak times.
